text.source_text: इतश्च परमेव ब्रह्म द्युभ्वाद्यायतनम् ; यस्मान्मुक्तोपसृप्यतास्य व्यपदिश्यमाना दृश्यते । मुक्तैरुपसृप्यं मुक्तोपसृप्यम् । देहादि ष्वनात्मसु अहमस्मीत्यात्मबुद्धिरविद्या , ततस्तत्पूजनादौ रागः , तत्परिभवादौ द्वेषः , तदुच्छेददर्शनाद्भयं मोहश्च — इत्येवमयमनन्तभेदोऽनर्थव्रातः सन्ततः सर्वेषां नः प्रत्यक्षः । तद्विपर्यये णाविद्यारागद्वेषादिदोषमुक्तैरुपसृप्यं गम्यमेतदिति द्युभ्वाद्यायतनं प्रकृत्य व्यपदेशो भवति । कथम् ? ‘ भिद्यते हृदयग्रन्थिश्छिद्यन्ते सर्वसंशयाः । क्षीयन्ते चास्य कर्माणि तस्मिन्दृष्टे परावरे ’ (मु. उ. २ । २ । ९) इत्युक्त्वा , ब्रवीति — ‘ तथा विद्वा न्नामरूपाद्विमुक्तः परात्परं पुरुषमुपैति दिव्यम् ’ (मु. उ. ३ । २ । ८) इति । ब्रह्मणश्च मुक्तोपसृप्यत्वं प्रसिद्धं शास्त्रे — ‘ यदा सर्वे प्रमुच्यन्ते कामा येऽस्य हृदि श्रिताः । अथ मर्त्योऽमृतो भवत्यत्र ब्रह्म समश्नुते ’ (बृ. उ. ४ । ४ । ७) इत्येवमादौ । प्रधानादीनां तु न क्वचिन्मुक्तोपसृप्यत्वमस्ति प्रसिद्धम् । अपि च ‘ तमेवैकं जानथ आत्मानमन्या वाचो विमुञ्चथामृतस्यैष सेतुः ’ इति वाग्विमोकपूर्वकं विज्ञेयत्वमिह द्युभ्वाद्यायतनस्योच्यते । तच्च श्रुत्यन्तरे ब्रह्मणो दृष्टम् — ‘ तमेव धीरो विज्ञाय प्रज्ञां कुर्वीत ब्राह्मणः । ना नुध्यायाद्बहूञ्शब्दान्वाचो विग्लापनं हि तत् ’ (बृ. उ. ४ । ४ । २१) इति । तस्मादपि द्युभ्वाद्यायतनं परं ब्रह्म ॥ २ ॥
text.transliteration_iast: itaśca parameva brahma dyubhvādyāyatanam ; yasmānmuktopasṛpyatāsya vyapadiśyamānā dṛśyate | muktairupasṛpyaṃ muktopasṛpyam | dehādi ṣvanātmasu ahamasmītyātmabuddhiravidyā , tatastatpūjanādau rāgaḥ , tatparibhavādau dveṣaḥ , taducchedadarśanādbhayaṃ mohaśca — ityevamayamanantabhedo'narthavrātaḥ santataḥ sarveṣāṃ naḥ pratyakṣaḥ | tadviparyaye ṇāvidyārāgadveṣādidoṣamuktairupasṛpyaṃ gamyametaditi dyubhvādyāyatanaṃ prakṛtya vyapadeśo bhavati | katham ? ‘ bhidyate hṛdayagranthiśchidyante sarvasaṃśayāḥ | kṣīyante cāsya karmāṇi tasmindṛṣṭe parāvare ’ (mu. u. 2 | 2 | 9) ityuktvā , bravīti — ‘ tathā vidvā nnāmarūpādvimuktaḥ parātparaṃ puruṣamupaiti divyam ’ (mu. u. 3 | 2 | 8) iti | brahmaṇaśca muktopasṛpyatvaṃ prasiddhaṃ śāstre — ‘ yadā sarve pramucyante kāmā ye'sya hṛdi śritāḥ | atha martyo'mṛto bhavatyatra brahma samaśnute ’ (bṛ. u. 4 | 4 | 7) ityevamādau | pradhānādīnāṃ tu na kvacinmuktopasṛpyatvamasti prasiddham | api ca ‘ tamevaikaṃ jānatha ātmānamanyā vāco vimuñcathāmṛtasyaiṣa setuḥ ’ iti vāgvimokapūrvakaṃ vijñeyatvamiha dyubhvādyāyatanasyocyate | tacca śrutyantare brahmaṇo dṛṣṭam — ‘ tameva dhīro vijñāya prajñāṃ kurvīta brāhmaṇaḥ | nā nudhyāyādbahūñśabdānvāco viglāpanaṃ hi tat ’ (bṛ. u. 4 | 4 | 21) iti | tasmādapi dyubhvādyāyatanaṃ paraṃ brahma || 2 ||

translation.literal_translation: Further, that support must be supreme Brahman because it is stated as attained by the liberated. What is reached by the liberated is called 'that which is approached by the free.' Ignorance is taking non-self such as body as self; from that come attachment, aversion, fear, and delusion, producing endless suffering known directly to all. Opposite to that, this reality is described as reached by those free from ignorance and its defects. How? After saying 'the knot of the heart is cut, all doubts are cut, his karmas are exhausted when That is seen,' scripture says: 'thus the knower, freed from name and form, reaches the supreme shining Person beyond the beyond.' Scripture is well known for Brahman as what the liberated attain: when all desires lodged in the heart are released, the mortal becomes immortal and attains Brahman here. No such liberated-attainability is established for Pradhana and the like. Also the knowability of this support is taught together with release from distracting speech, and this pattern is seen elsewhere in Brahman-teaching: knowing That, the wise should cultivate understanding and not brood on many words. Therefore too the support of heaven and earth is supreme Brahman.
translation.technical_translation: Argument from muktopasrpyavyapadesa: the text links the referent to liberation-fruits (knot-cutting, doubt-destruction, karma-exhaustion, name-form transcendence), which are canonical Brahman markers and unavailable to Pradhana alternatives.
translation.interpretive_note: Liberation-teleology is used as decisive referent filter.

lexical_notes.contextual_sense: Tracks each block inside the section-level inferential sequence

A later philological pass may tighten lexical precision for setu/ayatana/atacchabda terminology.
